title: Founder-Market Fit

introduction.subtitle: Strategic fit assessment. 7-9 min.
introduction.description:
Founder-Market Fit helps you learn the vocabulary and typical expectations behind Domain Edge, Customer Access, Credibility & Assets, Long-Term Pull & Learning. Use it as a private diagnostic: answer from observable evidence, not aspiration, and treat the result as a map for the next improvement cycle.

This educational quiz runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: How directly have you worked on the target problem?

options.text: A. This is not explicit yet in Domain Edge; decisions depend on intuition, habit, or one-off judgement.
B. There is a plausible practice in Domain Edge, but evidence, ownership, or repeatability is still partial.
C. Domain Edge has a documented practice with examples, owners, and current evidence used in decisions.
D. Domain Edge is reviewed, measured over time, and strong enough to guide trade-offs under pressure.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Domain Edge: How directly have you worked on the target problem
conceptHelp.summary: Domain Edge means the founder advantage that comes from lived experience, uncommon insight, or deep exposure to the target problem. This question narrows the concept to "How directly have you worked on the target problem?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Domain Edge matters because founders, investors, partners, and teams need to know whether this area can guide decisions under pressure, not just sound good in a plan.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable evidence: decisions, customer conversations, metrics, owners, records, reviews, policies, deal materials, or shipped work. Choose a high answer only when the evidence has changed a real choice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show how the team answers "How directly have you worked on the target problem?" today, who owns it, what evidence supports it, and one recent decision or trade-off it changed.

questions.text: How quickly can you reach qualified target users for discovery?

options.text: A. It happens rarely, reactively, or only when a crisis forces attention.
B. It happens occasionally, but timing depends on individual initiative or urgency.
C. There is a defined cadence with clear participants, owners, and triggers.
D. The cadence is frequent enough to change decisions, includes quality checks, and improves from outcomes.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Customer Access: How quickly can you reach qualified target users for discovery
conceptHelp.summary: Customer Access means how quickly and credibly a founder can reach the right users, buyers, partners, or market insiders for learning and traction. This question narrows the concept to "How quickly can you reach qualified target users for discovery?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Customer Access matters because founders, investors, partners, and teams need to know whether this area can guide decisions under pressure, not just sound good in a plan.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable evidence: decisions, customer conversations, metrics, owners, records, reviews, policies, deal materials, or shipped work. Choose a high answer only when the evidence has changed a real choice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show how the team answers "How quickly can you reach qualified target users for discovery?" today, who owns it, what evidence supports it, and one recent decision or trade-off it changed.

questions.text: How strong is your access to buyers, channel partners, or market influencers?

options.text: A. The answer is broad, assumed, or based mostly on internal belief.
B. There is a high-level description, but segmentation, trigger, or buyer evidence is thin.
C. The answer is defined for a specific segment with documented behaviour, alternatives, and decision criteria.
D. The answer is validated across repeated cases and directly informs targeting, messaging, prioritisation, and trade-offs.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: What proof makes target customers trust you?

options.text: A. The answer relies mainly on opinion, pitch, memory, or isolated examples.
B. There are supporting examples, but sources are partial, informal, or hard to compare.
C. There is documented evidence from relevant customers, operations, metrics, or records.
D. The evidence is triangulated, current, owner-reviewed, and strong enough to change a material decision.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Credibility & Assets: What proof makes target customers trust you
conceptHelp.summary: Credibility & Assets means the signals, proof, relationships, rights, data, or capabilities that make customers and partners believe this team can deliver. This question narrows the concept to "What proof makes target customers trust you?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.

options.text: A. This is not explicit yet in Long-Term Pull & Learning; decisions depend on intuition, habit, or one-off judgement.

This question narrows the concept to "How durable is your motivation to work on this problem?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.

questions.text: How deeply do you understand the hidden workflow around the problem?

options.text: A. No concrete example shows hidden customer workflow knowledge; the answer relies on intention, memory, or individual judgement.
B. There are isolated examples of hidden customer workflow knowledge, but they are inconsistent, hard to verify, or depend on one person.
C. Recent examples show hidden customer workflow knowledge in practice, with enough detail to judge quality and repeatability.
D. Hidden customer workflow knowledge is repeatedly demonstrated under pressure and changes decisions, ownership, or trade-offs.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Domain Edge: How deeply do you understand the hidden workflow around the problem
conceptHelp.summary: Domain Edge is tested here through a concrete operating question: "How deeply do you understand the hidden workflow around the problem?". Use the answer choices to separate aspiration from current practice.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Domain Edge matters because weak practice in this area can make the whole result look more mature than the operating system really is.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable behaviour: artifacts, decisions, customer evidence, owners, reviews, or shipped work. Do not score intention as practice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show current evidence, a named owner, and one recent decision changed by this practice.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
